about.metaDescription: Learn about Dociva — free, fast, and secure online file tools for PDFs, images, video, and text. No registration required.
about.title: About Dociva
about.missionText: Dociva was built to make professional-grade file tools accessible to everyone — for free. We believe that converting, compressing, and editing documents should be simple, fast, and private. No sign-ups, no hidden fees, no software to install.
about.technologyText: Our platform is powered by modern cloud infrastructure designed for speed and reliability. Files are processed server-side using industry-leading open-source libraries, ensuring accurate conversions and high-quality output every time. Our AI-powered tools leverage advanced machine learning models for intelligent document analysis, translation, and summarization.
about.securityText: Security is built into every layer of our platform. All file transfers are encrypted via HTTPS. Uploaded files are validated, size-limited, and processed in isolated environments. We employ rate limiting and input sanitization to prevent abuse.
about.privacyText: Your files belong to you. We never read, share, or sell the content of your documents. All uploaded and processed files are automatically deleted from our servers within {{minutes}} minutes. No human eyes ever see your data — processing is fully automated.
toolCategories: PDF tools — convert, compress, merge, split, rotate, watermark, protect, unlock, edit, and more
Image tools — convert, resize, compress, remove backgrounds with AI
AI document tools — chat with PDFs, summarize, translate, extract tables
OCR — extract text from images and scanned PDFs in English, Arabic, and French
Utility tools — QR code generator, video to GIF, word counter, text cleaner

pricing.metaDescription: Compare Free and Pro plans, understand how Dociva credits work, and see why heavier workloads can cost more than lightweight tasks.
pricing.title: Plans & Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Start free, track credits clearly, and upgrade only when you need more processing capacity.
pricing.faq1q: Is the Free plan really free?
pricing.faq1a: Yes. Free accounts receive 50 credits every 30 days, and all core tools remain available without a credit card.
pricing.faq2q: Can I cancel the Pro plan anytime?
pricing.faq2a: Yes. Cancel anytime and your account returns to the Free plan at the end of the current billing period.
pricing.faq3q: What payment methods do you accept?
pricing.faq3a: We accept major credit and debit cards through Stripe. Payments are processed securely without exposing your card data to Dociva.
pricing.transparencyTitle: See exactly how credits are counted
pricing.transparencyBody: Read the full explanation of fixed vs dynamic pricing, pre-run quotes, and why we price by workload instead of a rigid transaction count.

faq.q: Is the free plan really free?
faq.a: Yes! You get full access to all 30+ tools with generous daily limits — no credit card required.

faq.q: Can I cancel the Pro plan anytime?
faq.a: Absolutely. Cancel anytime with no questions asked. You'll keep Pro access until the end of your billing period.

faq.q: What payment methods do you accept?
faq.a: We accept all major credit/debit cards and PayPal. All payments are securely processed via Stripe.

pricing.trustTitle: Built for teams that need speed and predictability
pricing.trustSubtitle: The same platform supports quick browser tasks, recurring account usage, and a credit model designed to reflect actual processing workload.
pricing.trustFastDesc: Async task handling and optimized workers keep heavy jobs moving without blocking the interface.
pricing.trustPrivateDesc: Uploads are validated, processed securely, and cleaned up automatically after the retention window.
pricing.trustApiDesc: Pro workspaces can generate API keys and connect the same tools to internal automations and client flows.

pricingTransparency.metaDescription: Understand how Dociva credits work, what stays fixed, what can vary by workload, and why credits are not tied to a fixed number of provider requests.
pricingTransparency.title: How credit pricing works at Dociva
pricingTransparency.subtitle: We want your balance to reflect real processing effort, not an arbitrary transaction counter. This page explains what is fixed, what can vary, and what you see before you run a task.
pricingTransparency.creditsBody: Free and Pro accounts receive a credit allocation for each 30-day window. Your balance decreases only when a task is accepted.
pricingTransparency.quoteTitle: You see the price before heavy jobs run
pricingTransparency.quoteBody: For supported heavy and AI tools, Dociva can show a quote before execution so you can decide with context.
pricingTransparency.fairnessTitle: Not every task should cost the same
pricingTransparency.fairnessBody: Merging a PDF and translating a long document do not consume the same resources. Credit pricing reflects that difference.
pricingTransparency.howBody: Light and medium tools usually keep a fixed credit cost. Heavy and AI tools can use a dynamic quote based on the file size or processing workload expected before the task is queued.
pricingTransparency.fixedBody: Tasks like simple PDF edits or small conversions keep a predictable, low cost so common workflows stay easy to budget.
pricingTransparency.dynamicBody: Resource-heavy tasks such as OCR, compression, or AI-assisted document workflows may cost more when the file or workload is larger.
pricingTransparency.noteTitle: What we want to be explicit about
pricingTransparency.noteBody: Your site credits are a product balance for Dociva usage. They are not a direct token meter from our infrastructure providers.
pricingTransparency.noOneToOneBody: There is no fixed rule such as "1 credit = X OpenRouter requests." Some jobs make one provider call, some make several, and some AI-priced workflows do not rely on OpenRouter at all.
pricingTransparency.exampleLightBody: A lightweight tool such as merging or rotating a PDF stays near the minimum price because the processing burden is low and predictable.
pricingTransparency.exampleHeavyBody: OCR, compression, or background removal may cost more because file size and computation time increase the real workload.
pricingTransparency.exampleAiBody: Chat, summarization, or translation start from a higher base because they involve language processing and, in some cases, multiple provider calls.
pricingTransparency.futureBody: Today the product still uses Free and Pro plans, but the pricing philosophy already aims at fairer usage: easier tasks should consume less balance than expensive ones. That is the foundation for future recharge flexibility.
pricingTransparency.faq1q: Will I always know the cost in advance?
pricingTransparency.faq1a: For supported heavy and AI tools, Dociva can show a pre-run quote before dispatch. Simpler tools keep a fixed credit cost.
pricingTransparency.faq2q: What happens when my balance is too low?
pricingTransparency.faq2a: The task is rejected before processing starts, so you can review your balance or upgrade without spending credits blindly.
pricingTransparency.faq3q: Why not price everything as one transaction?
pricingTransparency.faq3a: A flat transaction count is easier to explain but less fair. Workload-aware pricing helps keep simple tools affordable without underpricing expensive jobs.

Fast cloud-based conversion — no software to install
Works on any device with a modern browser
Files auto-deleted after 30 minutes for privacy
useCases: Editing scanned contracts or agreements
Updating resumes saved as PDF
Reusing content from academic papers or reports
Making changes to legal or government forms
Extracting text from PDF brochures

faq.q: How do I convert a PDF to Word?
faq.a: Upload your PDF file to our converter and it will automatically convert it to an editable Word document (DOCX). Click download when the conversion is complete.

faq.a: Yes, our PDF to Word converter is completely free with no hidden costs or signup required.

faq.q: Will my PDF formatting be preserved?
faq.a: Our converter preserves the original formatting, fonts, and layout of your PDF as closely as possible in the Word output.

faq.q: Is my file secure?
faq.a: All uploaded files are automatically deleted from our servers within 30 minutes. We never share or store your documents.

faq.q: What is the maximum file size?
faq.a: You can upload PDF files up to 20MB for conversion.

useCases: Sharing reports with clients in a universal format

Creating print-ready documents from Word files

Preparing files for digital signatures

faq.q: How do I convert Word to PDF?
faq.a: Upload your Word file (.doc or .docx) and our converter will instantly create a high-quality PDF while preserving all formatting.

faq.q: Can I convert DOC files, not just DOCX?
faq.a: Yes, both older DOC format and modern DOCX format are fully supported.

faq.q: Will images and tables be included?
faq.a: Yes, all images, tables, headers, footers, and graphical elements in your Word document will be faithfully reproduced in the PDF.

faq.q: Is there a file size limit?
faq.a: You can convert Word files up to 20MB in size.

compressPdf.whatItDoes: Reduce the file size of your PDF documents by up to 90% while keeping them readable and high quality. Choose between three compression levels to balance quality and file size according to your needs.
howToUse: Upload your PDF file to the compressor.

Ideal for email attachments and uploads
useCases: Making large PDFs small enough to email
Reducing storage space for archived documents
Speeding up PDF uploads to websites
Optimizing PDFs for mobile viewing
Preparing documents for web publishing

faq.q: How does PDF compression work?
faq.a: Our tool optimizes images, removes unnecessary metadata, and compresses internal structures to reduce file size while maintaining visual quality.

faq.q: Will compression affect text quality?
faq.a: No. Text remains crisp and searchable. Mainly images within the PDF are optimized to reduce file size.

faq.q: How much can I reduce my PDF size?
faq.a: Depending on the content, you can typically reduce file size by 50-90%, especially for PDFs with many images.

faq.q: Is there a file size limit?

Separating a multi-page scanned document
